question.text: What happens when the risk categorization made by the Financial Group entity differs from that of the Regulated Entity for CDD updation periodicity?

answer.text: The stricter of the two periodicities shall apply
The Regulated Entity's own categorization always prevails
The Financial Group entity's categorization always prevails
IFSCA must be consulted to resolve the difference

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Where the risk categorization made by the Financial Group entity differs from the risk categorization made by the Regulated Entity, the stricter of the two periodicity shall apply. This ensures the higher standard of vigilance is maintained.
**The Trap:** It's not about which entity has more authority — it's about which standard is STRICTER (more frequent updation). The conservative approach always wins in AML/CFT compliance.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Financial Group-level compliance coordination is important in IFSCA-01 because many IFSC entities are part of larger financial groups. This concept may not appear in Series XXIV which focuses on individual intermediaries.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When in doubt, apply the stricter standard. If your RE says medium-risk (3 years) but the Financial Group says high-risk (1 year), update annually. This principle applies broadly across AML/CFT compliance.
